vitals.terrain: mountains, high passes, forests, back roads
vitals.surface: mostly paved roads with short gravel sections

course_description.overview: Self-supported bikepacking race through Western Carpathians, crossing Poland, Czech Republic, Slovakia, Germany, Austria, Hungary, and Romania, with free route choice between 4 control points, featuring high mountain passes, deep forests, long climbs, and quiet back roads.

key_segments.name: Opening parcours
key_segments.description: From Nowy Targ into Western Carpathians to Slovakia via cycle routes, short gravel, secondary roads, High Tatras views, and classic climbs to Poprad Valley.

key_segments.name: Control Point 01 - Kudłacze
key_segments.description: Crossroads in Beskids with five parcours options emerging in mountain hut.

key_segments.name: Biele Karpaty section
key_segments.description: Rolling hills through protected landscape area, smallest paved roads below Veľká Javorina (970m), decisions at summit control point.

key_segments.name: Southern slopes and passes
key_segments.description: Tarmac mountain roads through untouched nature, high struggle but pristine joy.

key_segments.name: Spiš Loop
key_segments.description: Back to Poland, beautiful cycling route to finish in Nowy Targ.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Extreme: 1800km with 27,000m elevation in one stage, self-supported navigation, sleep, food management through demanding Carpathian terrain and personal route decisions.

logistics.getting_there: Start in Nowy Targ, Poland, next to highest-located airport in Poland; international access via nearby airports.
logistics.accommodation: Self-supported; riders manage own sleep; control points offer stamps, potential rest spots like mountain huts or hotels.
logistics.registration_details: Registration open (past editions from November); check racethroughpoland.pl for No.8 details.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bikepacking setup required; suitable for bikes handling paved roads and short gravel; self-managed equipment.
logistics.local_considerations: Mountain weather variability; navigation skills important; regions with low traffic but unforgiving climbs; highlander hospitality at finish.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic self-supported bikepacking odyssey through Carpathian mountains across 7 countries.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-endurance bikepackers seeking raw adventure, navigation challenges, and stunning multi-country scenery.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those craving a true test of self-reliance in one of Europe's most scenic mountain ranges.
pro_tips: Master navigation apps for free-route choices between controls.
Pack for variable mountain weather and self-sufficiency.
Prioritize climbs in Tatras and Beskids for efficiency.
Embrace decisions at control points to shape your race.

videos.title: Discover Colorado's Gold Hill Inn and Bluebird Lodge
videos.description: Gold Hill was the first permanent mining camp in the Colorado mountains. Today, the tiny town is a gem of a community, filled history and home to a rustic and welcoming lodge and restaurant.
videos.transcript:
back in 1859 Prospectors panned their
way at Four Mile Creek in about 11 miles
outside of Boulder they struck gold
welcome to Gold Hill Colorado this is
actually the first permanent mining camp
in all of Colorado's mountains and today
home to a historic and welcoming Inn and
Restaurant
[Music]
it's probably one of the more unique
places in Colorado it's real Colorado
it's real Colorado even before Colorado
became a state Gold Hill was binding
District number one in the territory of
Nebraska Mining and padding for gold was
the name of the game and people started
flocking and settling here because this
is where all the action was back then
miners built a settlement of log houses
followed by stores in a school then more
lodging was needed the Wentworth House
was built 1872 as a fancy tourist Hotel
a dining hall was built next door on the
bluebird Lodge ran through the 1950s
that's until Barbara and Frank Finn fell
in love with the space and purchased the
property for twelve thousand dollars the
lodge and restaurant opened in the 1960s
under the name Gold Hill Inn now run by
Brothers Ryan and Chris try to keep it
in the tradition our parents started it
in Chris's head chef electrician plumber
pretty much does it all everything is is
made
every day it's here we just we like food
we like to make food he calls his
Cuisine Mountain Gourmet and it's a full
dining experience the menu in the main
dining room is what we call a toppledote
which means it's everything included
it's a six course meal or a three-course
meal Brian will greet you at the door
and tell you all about the evening's
offerings
soups and entree of choice dessert
cheese and fruit it makes for a full
evening and it's slowing down and
relaxing everybody needs to do that
every once in a while you can also enjoy
the menu a la carte out in the bar we
get Denver diners we get Fort Collins
diners we got a fair amount of tourism
in the spring and summer the beer garden
is a great place to enjoy a cold one and
on Friday nights listen to some of the
best Bluegrass in Colorado the lodge can
be rented out as a whole for weddings or
family reunions when you walk in the
front door if you have never been here
before you'll know why you made the trip
up in a world that can get so hectic
this town provides quiet Gold Hill Inn
is an honest representation of
preservation not only will the journey
to Gold Hill provide some stunning
Mountain views and a little break from
the hustle and bustle but you'll be
transported back in time where log
cabins ruled and Hearty family meals
healed we think Gold Hill Inn is well
worth discovering for Denver 7's
discover Colorado I'm Katie LaSalle

videos.title: Na tym rowerze ściga sie jeden z najlepszych sprinterów świata!
videos.description: Marcel Kittel z Giant-Alpecin to jeden z najlepszych sprinterów świata. W niedziele wygrał pierwszy etap na tegorocznej edycji Tour De Pologne. Ile wazy taki rower?
